commit_scope: Make Hostinger deploy SSH setup resilient when ssh-keyscan times out repeatedly.

local_validation.notes: Two Hostinger deploy attempts in this thread failed only in Configure SSH because ssh-keyscan timed out for 187.77.152.42. The workflow now keeps the existing keyscan path and falls back to OpenSSH accept-new for the configured host after the retry budget is exhausted.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

deploy_validation.notes: Pending merge and a manually dispatched Hostinger deploy to settle latest main SHA parity.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Hostinger deploy no longer fails before SSH/scp solely because ssh-keyscan timed out. The actual SSH connection still verifies or records the host key with accept-new and will fail if the host is unreachable.
test_flows: Hostinger Auto Deploy workflow validates as YAML.
Hostinger Auto Deploy is manually dispatched for latest main after merge.

phase_gate.reason: The repeated SSH setup blocker is patched locally. CI, merge, deploy, and public verification are still pending.

evidence_refs: Hostinger Auto Deploy run 26758485202 initially failed on ssh-keyscan and passed on rerun.
Hostinger Auto Deploy run 26759693738 failed on ssh-keyscan again for the latest main SHA.
.github/workflows/hostinger-auto-deploy.yml now falls back to StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new after keyscan retries.
